events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes a field-service visit where the observed condition is "reversed polarity" on receptacles & switches. The work is at the "rough-complete" stage.

Reversed polarity on a receptacle means the hot and neutral wires are swapped. This is a code violation under NEC 406.4 (which covers receptacles) - specifically, receptacles must be properly wired with correct polarity. NEC 406.9 covers faceplates, but 406.4 is the key reference here for receptacle installation requirements.

Reversed polarity is a safety hazard - it can cause appliances to have energized parts that should be neutral, creating shock hazards.

Decision: fail (non-compliant and potentially dangerous)
Risk: high (reversed polarity creates shock hazards)
Work stage: rough-complete
Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant
Percent complete: rough-complete would be maybe 80-90%? Rough-complete means installed but not closed out. I'll estimate around 85%.
Value: 0 (no specific numeric reading requested)
Confidence: high, maybe 0.95
Findings: reversed polarity on receptacle(s)
Actions: correct wiring to restore proper polarity, verify with tester
Rationale: reversed polarity violates NEC requirements for proper receptacle wiring
References: NEC 406.4, NEC 210.8, NEC 406.9
